component.name: Verbuno
component.summary: Native Linux translation through your own AI provider

description.p: Verbuno is a lightweight C++ and Qt 6 Widgets translation client that stays available from the Linux system tray.
It connects directly to OpenRouter or another OpenAI-compatible endpoint selected by the user. It has no telemetry and stores API keys only in the desktop keychain.

ul.li: Translate between a broad catalog of languages with streamed output.
Extract editable text from opened, pasted or dropped photos using local Tesseract OCR.
Choose any model or refresh the current OpenRouter free-model catalog.
Restrict OpenRouter routing by data-collection and Zero Data Retention policies.
Keep local history only when explicitly enabled.

description.p: Made local preferences verifiably persistent, enabled secure remembered API keys by default, and added visible storage and credential status.
Added asynchronous local photo OCR with image preview, installed-language discovery, layout modes, confidence feedback and an explicit review-before-translation workflow.
Renamed the application to Verbuno, removed the optional context field, and added the actual routed OpenRouter provider and model to the interface and local history.
Added a standard system-decorated window, visible provider and model information, and runtime English, Russian, Ukrainian and German interfaces.
Initial native Linux release with tray popup, OpenRouter streaming, secure key storage, privacy routing, optional local history, and native packages.
